item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A begins by agreeing that social interests should take priority, then shifts toward Agent B's rights-protection framing and explicitly endorses Agent B's argument at turn 5. Agent B begins with a balanced rights-protection critique, strengthens it at turn 4, then moves back toward balance at turn 6 by warning that excessive protection of individual liberties can hinder social progress. Agent B turn 4 contains non-Latin script mixed into an English response.
